title: Call-Out
text: Call-Out Uniface components can activate non-Uniface components, a process known as call-out. Uniface components can call out to non-Uniface components with activate and newinstance ProcScript statements. The middleware driver is able to translate and route these statements to the non-Uniface environment. In this way, instances of non-Uniface components can be created, operations can be invoked, data can be passed, and, if necessary, the non-Uniface component instances can be deleted. Mapping information that might be required can be defined in the Signature Editor. At runtime, Uniface uses signatures to identify components and their interfaces. Related Links Call-In Non-Uniface environments can call in to Uniface components. All communication is passed to the Uniface components, making it possible to create and delete instances of the Uniface components, invoke their operations, and pass data to and from the components. Signatures A Uniface signature describes the remote interface of a component. The interface enables other components to address the component without knowing anything about the implementation. This allows components of different origin to communicate through their respective interfaces. Signature Editor The Signature Editor enables you to define, edit, and compile component signatures for both Uniface and non-Uniface components. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
